subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Area Between Curves, Volume of Solid & Related Rates of Cross-Section
stimulus: Let $f$ and $g$ be the functions defined by $f(x) = \ln(x+3)$ and $g(x) = x^4 + 2x^3$. The graphs of $f$ and $g$ intersect at $x = -2$ and $x = B$, where $B > 0$.

parts.prompt: Find the area of the region enclosed by the graphs of $f$ and $g$.
parts.scoring_notes: Second point requires lower limit $-2$ and upper limit as letter $B$ or a value accurate to at least one decimal between $0$ and $1$. If $g(x) - f(x)$ used, must resolve the negative sign to earn third point. Must earn first point to be eligible for third.

scoring_points.official_rubric: The first point requires the integrand $f(x) - g(x)$. If $g(x) - f(x)$ is used, the student must resolve the negative sign to earn the third (answer) point.

sample_responses.commentary: Correct top-minus-bottom integrand — $f(x) \ge g(x)$ on $[-2, B]$, so $f - g$ gives the area directly.
Uses $g - f$; eligible for the first integrand point, but the student must account for the sign to earn the answer point.
Equivalent split form — the difference of two integrals with correct limits earns the integrand point.

scoring_points.description: Correct limits: lower limit $-2$, upper limit $B \approx 0.782$

required_elements: Correct limits: lower limit $-2$, upper limit $B \approx 0.782$

scoring_points.official_rubric: The second point requires lower limit $-2$ and upper limit $B$ (as a letter) or a decimal value accurate to at least one decimal between $0$ and $1$ ($B \approx 0.782$).

sample_responses.commentary: Uses the numerically computed upper limit $B \approx 0.782$ with lower limit $-2$ — correct.
One-decimal approximation is acceptable for the upper limit.
Incorrect lower limit — the enclosed region starts at the left intersection $x = -2$, not $x = 0$.

scoring_points.official_rubric: The third point requires the correct numerical answer $\approx 3.604$ (or $3.603$). Must earn the first (integrand) point to be eligible. If $g - f$ was used, the absolute value must be taken or the sign resolved.

sample_responses.commentary: Correct integral with correct answer to three decimal places.
Insufficient precision — the answer must be accurate to at least three decimal places.

parts.prompt: For $-2 \le x \le B$, let $h(x)$ be the vertical distance between the graphs of $f$ and $g$. Is $h$ increasing or decreasing at $x = -0.5$? Give a reason for your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: If a value of $h'(-0.5)$ is given, it must be correct for digits reported. The last line alone ('$h'(-0.5) < 0$ so decreasing') earns both points. Presenting only $h'(x)$ without evaluation does not earn either point.

scoring_points.description: Considers $h'(x) = f'(x) - g'(x)$ or computes $h'(-0.5) = f'(-0.5) - g'(-0.5) \approx -0.600$

required_elements: Considers $h'(x) = f'(x) - g'(x)$ or computes $h'(-0.5) = f'(-0.5) - g'(-0.5) \approx -0.600$

scoring_points.official_rubric: The first point is earned for writing $h'(x) = f'(x) - g'(x)$ or for directly computing $h'(-0.5)$. Presenting only $h'(x)$ without evaluating at $x = -0.5$ does not earn either point.

sample_responses.commentary: Derives $h'$ and evaluates at $x = -0.5$ — value is negative, supporting the conclusion.
Direct computation at the required point without first writing the general formula — acceptable.
Only the general formula without evaluation at $x = -0.5$ — does not earn either point per the scoring notes.

scoring_points.official_rubric: The second point requires concluding that $h$ is decreasing at $x = -0.5$ with justification citing $h'(-0.5) < 0$. The last line '$h'(-0.5) < 0$, so decreasing' alone earns both points.

sample_responses.commentary: Correct conclusion with explicit citation of the negative derivative value.
Correct conclusion but no justification — must cite $h'(-0.5) < 0$.

parts.prompt: The region enclosed by the graphs of $f$ and $g$ is the base of a solid. Cross sections of the solid taken perpendicular to the $x$-axis are squares. Find the volume of the solid.
parts.scoring_notes: First point earned for integrand $k(f(x)-g(x))^2$ with $k \ne 0$; if $k \ne 1$, ineligible for second point. Presentation error (mismatched parentheses, misplaced exponent) does not earn first point but earns second for correct answer.

wrong_examples: does not earn first point but earns second for correct answer
scoring_points.official_rubric: The first point requires the integrand $(f(x) - g(x))^2$ — the side length of each square cross-section is the vertical distance between the curves. An integrand $k(f(x)-g(x))^2$ with $k \ne 1$ earns the first point but is ineligible for the answer point. A presentation error (mismatched parentheses, misplaced exponent) loses the first point but may still earn the answer point.

sample_responses.commentary: Square cross-section area = side$^2$ = $(f(x) - g(x))^2$ — correct integrand.
Uses $\pi(\cdots)^2$ — that formula is for a disk/washer (circular cross-sections), not square cross-sections.

wrong_examples: does not earn first point but earns second for correct answer
scoring_points.official_rubric: The second point requires the correct numerical answer $\approx 5.340$. A presentation error in the integrand that yields the correct answer still earns this point.

sample_responses.commentary: Correct answer to three decimal places.
Only two decimal places — insufficient precision.

parts.prompt: A vertical line in the $xy$-plane travels from left to right along the base of the solid described in part (c). The vertical line is moving at a constant rate of $7$ units per second. Find the rate of change of the area of the cross section above the vertical line with respect to time when the vertical line is at position $x = -0.5$.
parts.scoring_notes: Cross-section area $A(x) = (f(x)-g(x))^2$. $A'(x) = 2(f(x)-g(x))(f'(x)-g'(x))$. First point earned for presenting $\frac{dA}{dx}\cdot\frac{dx}{dt}$, $A'(x)\cdot 7$, or equivalent. Second point: correct answer $-9.272$ or $-9.271$.

scoring_points.official_rubric: The first point is earned for presenting the chain rule relationship $\frac{dA}{dt} = \frac{dA}{dx}\cdot\frac{dx}{dt}$, or equivalently $A'(x) \cdot 7$.

sample_responses.commentary: Explicitly applies the chain rule, multiplying the spatial rate of change $A'(x)$ by the speed of the moving line $\frac{dx}{dt} = 7$.
Directly sets up the chain rule at $x = -0.5$ without writing the general form — acceptable.
Forgets to multiply by $\frac{dx}{dt} = 7$ — the chain rule requires both factors.

scoring_points.official_rubric: The second point requires the correct numerical answer $\approx -9.272$ (or $-9.271$) square units per second.

sample_responses.commentary: Correct product with correct sign and units.
Drops the negative sign — $A'(-0.5) < 0$ because the vertical gap between the curves is decreasing at $x = -0.5$.
